# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

Sure, here's an example of how you might implement this in PHP using the WP_User_Query class to manage users within WordPress (assuming your theme uses a custom query like wp_user()).  This will store password hashes instead of plain text and compare those when needed. We use bcrypt for storing, while comparing we hash each submitted value against our stored one in PHP:
```php?file=WordPress%20Plugin/wp-content/plugins/usersmanagerplugin/.htaccess&line=14^C
